description: Local regulations pertaining to noise levels in residential areas of Bonn, Germany. These regulations are needed to ensure the research facility operates within legal limits and to address potential community concerns about noise pollution. Intended audience: Facility and Safety Manager, Principal Investigator.
steps_to_find: Search the official website of the City of Bonn.
Contact the local building authority (Bauamt) in Bonn.
Consult with a local legal expert specializing in environmental regulations.
access_difficulty: Medium: Requires navigating the City of Bonn website or contacting local authorities.

document_name: University Hospital Bonn Research Ethics Guidelines
description: The official ethical guidelines and procedures for conducting research involving human subjects at the University Hospital Bonn. These guidelines are needed to ensure compliance with ethical standards and to obtain IRB approval for the research project. Intended audience: Principal Investigator, Study Coordinator.
steps_to_find: Contact the University Hospital Bonn's ethics committee (Ethik-Kommission).
Search the University Hospital Bonn's website for research ethics policies.
Consult with the University's legal department.
access_difficulty: Medium: Requires contacting the University Hospital Bonn or searching their website.

description: The current German laws implementing the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), which govern the collection, storage, and use of personal data. These laws are needed to ensure compliance with data privacy regulations and to protect the privacy of study participants. Intended audience: Data Engineer, Principal Investigator.
steps_to_find: Search the official website of the German Federal Commissioner for Data Protection and Freedom of Information (BfDI).
Consult with a legal expert specializing in data protection law in Germany.
Review the official GDPR documentation.
access_difficulty: Easy: Readily available online through official government websites.

description: The American Academy of Sleep Medicine (AASM) scoring manual, which provides standardized criteria for scoring sleep stages and sleep-related events. This manual is needed to ensure consistent and reliable scoring of polysomnography data. Intended audience: Sleep Neurophysiology Postdoctoral Researcher, Research Technicians.
steps_to_find: Purchase the AASM scoring manual from the AASM website.
Access the AASM scoring manual through a library or institutional subscription.
Consult with a certified sleep technologist for guidance on AASM scoring criteria.
access_difficulty: Medium: Requires purchase or subscription to the AASM scoring manual.

document_name: University Hospital Bonn Patient Recruitment Policies
description: The official policies and procedures for recruiting patients for research studies at the University Hospital Bonn. These policies are needed to ensure ethical and compliant recruitment practices. Intended audience: Study Coordinator, Principal Investigator.
steps_to_find: Contact the University Hospital Bonn's research administration office.
Search the University Hospital Bonn's website for patient recruitment policies.
Consult with the University's legal department.
access_difficulty: Medium: Requires contacting the University Hospital Bonn or searching their website.

document_name: Existing German Building Codes and Safety Regulations
description: Current building codes and safety regulations in Germany, specifically those applicable to residential properties and research facilities. These codes are needed to ensure the research facility meets all safety requirements and to obtain the necessary building permits. Intended audience: Facility and Safety Manager, Principal Investigator.
steps_to_find: Search the official website of the German Institute for Standardization (DIN).
Contact the local building authority (Bauamt) in Bonn.
Consult with a local architect or engineer specializing in building codes.
access_difficulty: Medium: Requires navigating German standards organizations or contacting local authorities.

document_name: Existing University Hospital Bonn Fire Safety Regulations
description: The official fire safety regulations and procedures for buildings affiliated with the University Hospital Bonn. These regulations are needed to ensure compliance with fire safety standards and to protect the safety of study participants and staff. Intended audience: Facility and Safety Manager, Principal Investigator.
steps_to_find: Contact the University Hospital Bonn's safety department.
Search the University Hospital Bonn's website for fire safety policies.
Consult with the University's legal department.
access_difficulty: Medium: Requires contacting the University Hospital Bonn or searching their website.

description: Documentation for the Brain Imaging Data Structure (BIDS) standard for EEG data, including specifications for data organization, metadata, and file formats. This documentation is needed to ensure that the collected data is stored in a standardized format that facilitates data sharing and collaboration. Intended audience: Data Engineer, Sleep Neurophysiology Postdoctoral Researcher.
steps_to_find: Search the official EEG-BIDS website.
Consult with experts in EEG data management and BIDS standards.
Review publications on EEG-BIDS implementation.
access_difficulty: Easy: Readily available online through the official EEG-BIDS website.

document_name: Existing Literature on Adaptive PSG Protocols
description: Scientific publications and research articles on adaptive polysomnography (PSG) protocols, which involve adjusting the intensity and duration of PSG monitoring based on individual participant characteristics or data patterns. This literature is needed to inform the development of a data-driven algorithm for scheduling enhanced PSG nights. Intended audience: Sleep Neurophysiology Postdoctoral Researcher, Principal Investigator.
steps_to_find: Search scientific databases such as PubMed, Scopus, and Web of Science.
Consult with experts in sleep medicine and PSG protocols.
Review conference proceedings and presentations on sleep research.
access_difficulty: Easy: Readily available through scientific databases and online resources.
